review_text:
building's exterior may look unassuming or even a lil spooky but the inside is really cozy and homely! lovely space, limited tables but plenty of seats

if you really want a table, they have a table booking system you can use to ensure you have one when you get there!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Nostalgia a plenty in this well preserved library that houses many childhood memories for some.

Located in the quiet side of Queenstown facing sky residences @ Dawson, short walk from Queenstown MRT, a cozy library to spend your time here.

2 levels served by a lift & stairs, lots of reading areas and I particularly enjoy the level 2 quiet reading room facing the greenery.

There are lots of nostalgia and nice touches to this library.

Take your time to explore, read, borrow or exchange. what a privilege!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
As of April 2026: It is a old school library with the kinda of nostalgia you get when you visited a library in the 2000's.

Most of the Library's floor space is dedicated to books with working space being rather limited. It's not very convenient to access either as you gotta walk abit from the MRT station and not much amenities nearby.

The tables tend to get booked out until like 7pm ish. With patrons playing musical chairs where someone who has a booking asks those who didn't make a booking to move.

Interesting mix of patrons, with elderly men being the majority and somehow quite a few expats.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Decent spot for variety, but not for those craving familiar Pakistani flavours
Visited Lau Pa Sat (Lay Pa Sat) recently in Singapore. It’s a lively and iconic hawker centre with plenty of options and a nice atmosphere, especially in the evening when the streets are closed off for dining.
We tried the halal stall and ordered chicken rice noodles, chicken basil vegetables, and tea tree something. While the portions were generous and the service was quick, the overall taste was quite different from what we expected. Everything had a strong seafood-like flavour profile (even the non-seafood items), which didn’t really appeal to us as I can’t stand sea food smell. It felt quite far from Pakistani taste preferences – the spices, seasoning, and balance were very different.
If you’re in the mood to explore local Singaporean or Southeast Asian hawker food and try new things, Lau Pa Sat is a great place with lots of variety. However, if you’re particular about your food and want something closer to Pakistani cuisine, this might not be the best choice.
Cleanliness was just fine for a busy hawker centre, and the pricing was reasonable. Good for tourists or casual explorers, but we probably won’t rush back.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: fantastic place to grab some food and/or beer. Famous for the skewers on the street, especially the chicken satay. Plenty of options to choose from, Indian, Arabic, Chinese, Singaporean, etc. There's something for everyone. Good ventilation and plenty of tables but the place is always packed and noisy (it doesn't take away the enjoyment). A must to visit.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: There's no need to write much about this place; you simply must stop in for a fantastic meal. There are plenty of street food joints offering a variety of Asian cuisines. Everything is perfectly seasoned, with plenty to choose from, so everyone should easily find something to their liking. And the prices are very affordable by Singaporean standards.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Lau Pa Sat: A must-visit for the lively night vibe!

I visited at 9 PM on a Friday, and it was absolutely crowded! The energy outside on Satay Street was incredible, with everyone enjoying grilled food in the open air. Inside the historic building, there are plenty of food stalls to choose from.

The Drinks:
One thing to watch out for is that there are very few drink stalls, so the lines are very long. I bought an Iced Mango Peach ($2.80), but I wouldn't recommend it. It was extremely sweet and didn't contain any fresh fruit—it tasted mostly like syrup. I suggest skipping the "juices" here if you want something fresh.

The Food & Service:
I tried the Thunder Tea Rice ($6.20). It was okay, though it didn't quite fit my personal taste. After eating, the service is efficient; you can either clear your own tray or wait for the staff who come by frequently to collect them.

The Verdict:
It is very crowded and noisy with loud music, but it’s a "must-visit" to experience the local Singaporean vibe. Just be prepared for the crowds!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Tried Na Na Curry at Lau Pa Sat because saw that they are awarded Michelin Bib Gourmand 10 years in a row!

Their chicken curry is rich, slightly creamy, and super comforting. The spices are well-balanced, not too heavy but still very satisfying.

The chicken is tender and soaks up the curry nicely, making every bite flavourful. Pairs really well with both the rice and roti prata!

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Buona Vista MRT Station is one of the most convenient and well-designed stations in Singapore. It serves as an interchange for the East-West Line (EWL) and the Circle Line (CCL), making it an essential hub for commuters traveling across different parts of the island.

The station is clean, spacious, and efficiently managed. Clear signage and well-maintained facilities make transferring between lines smooth and hassle-free.

Its location is a big plus—Buona Vista is close to key areas like one-north, where many tech and research companies are based, and the Star Vista shopping mall, which offers great dining and retail options.

During peak hours, it can get a little crowded, but the station handles the traffic well. Overall, a reliable and strategically located MRT station that’s great for daily commuting and exploring the area!

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Buona Vista MRT Station is a very clean and convenient interchange between the East–West Line and Circle Line. Transfers are easy, and the station has clear signs, lifts, and good accessibility. It’s also right next to The Star Vista and one-north, so there are plenty of food and shopping options nearby. A reliable and well-maintained station overall.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Great station - close to the Star Vista Mall where you can find food, drinks, Cold Storage. It serves Green & Yellow lines although it can be confusing to navigate from one to the other.
There are two restaurants, two tea places and convenience store at the station.
There are two bike stations (front and back).

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: There are no sheltered bike parking lots. Is all uncovered and exposed to the elements. If locking the bike at the walkway poles (outside of the mrt station mind you), the staff are so free that they have time to complain. It’s not even part of the mrt station. Perhaps y’all should rethink what are important matters in your job and what is just being a hindrance to people trying to live a healthy and eco friendly life.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
A crowded station with 2 MRT lines - the circle line and east west line. My husband and I observed the circle line area and feel that there are insufficient seating area at the circle line platform.

The station can be enhanced by putting up more seats at the circle line platforms to better serve the needs of commuters waiting for the trains.

review_text: We are very happy with this kiddiwinkie! The teachers are caring, patient, and truly dedicated to the children’s growth and well-being. My son looks forward to school every day and has become more  independent, and curious about learning. The environment is safe, clean!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
My daughter started at this school when she was 18 months old. I visited many schools, but I loved the environment here the most. The school is located in a quiet, standalone setting and offers plenty of outdoor activities every day, which I really appreciate.
Since joining, she has also started speaking Chinese, which has been wonderful to see. All the teachers are friendly, warm, and professional. I’m especially grateful to Teacher Zurah and Jia Xin Lao Shi for their care and dedication.
I truly feel thankful every day for this school and the positive impact it has had on my daughter.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Big thank you teacher Elsie and Lu Laoshi for their care and support of the children. The school is well organised with a lot of activities. My kid likes is happy to come to school and enjoys it very much. It s a safe and cosy school like a family ❤️ 👍

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
My 2-year-old daughter has been very well cared for by Teacher Shiho-san, Radiah, and Fang Ying Laoshi. Since she started preschool, we have noticed her growing interest in many things, her ability to learn new words, and her development into a brighter and more cheerful personality. We are truly grateful to the teachers for nurturing such wonderful growth in her.
In addition, Shiho-san’s Japanese class and the thoughtful daily communication have been very meaningful learning experiences for our daughter. We deeply appreciate all the dedication and support. Thank you very much!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
We’ve had such a positive experience with KiddieWinkie Newton. From day one, the teachers and staff made my child feel safe, loved, and supported. What really stood out to me was how much they focused on my child’s overall development — not just academically, but emotionally and socially too.

The teachers are incredibly attentive and always provide feedback on my child’s progress. I’ve noticed huge improvements in communication skills, confidence, and independence. They use creative play, structured learning, and even introduce early literacy and numeracy in a way that’s fun and engaging.

I also love how they encourage curiosity and help the kids build healthy relationships with their peers. My child comes home excited to share what they learned every day — that speaks volumes.

Thank you to the KiddieWinkie team for being such an important part of our child’s early years. Highly recommend this centre to any parent looking for a nurturing and developmental-focused environment!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Clementi Public Library: A great resource, but often crowded with non-readers.

I was very surprised to find a large library inside such a modern shopping mall. The space itself is spacious and clean, with a wide variety of books available.

The Atmosphere:
I visited at 7 PM and noticed that almost all the chairs were taken by people using their phones. It felt more like a place for people to sit and rest after shopping rather than a quiet space for reading or study. If you are looking for a peaceful corner to actually read, it might be difficult to find a seat here.

The Verdict:
The library has an excellent collection, but the atmosphere can feel a bit like a busy extension of the mall. It’s better for a quick "grab-and-go" book pick-up rather than a long, quiet reading session.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: One of the few libraries which bother to keep the patrons quiet. My family was very politely told off a while ago because my kids' flip flops were too loud. I think it is the right thing to do , but the libraries i went to when young. Gone are these days - u finds kids playing hide and seek or catching jn say woodlands library. So well done!!

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
HortPark has been around for more than a decade, and I’ve been coming here for many years — sometimes with family, sometimes for team-building activities, and sometimes just to enjoy a meal at one of the restaurants. It’s one of those places that quietly grows on you over time

What I love most is how versatile it is. The wide open lawns are perfect for kids to run around, the themed gardens are thoughtfully curated, and there are plenty of charming corners — from gazebos to shaded benches — where you can simply sit and relax. The greenery feels mature and well-established, giving the park a calm, lived-in character that newer parks sometimes lack

I’ve had meaningful team bonding sessions here, casual weekend strolls with family, and peaceful afternoons just soaking in the surroundings. It’s also conveniently connected to other green spaces, making it a great starting or ending point for a longer walk

The best time to visit is when the weather is kind — not too hot and not rainy. On breezy mornings or gentle late afternoons, HortPark truly shines. The combination of open lawns, beautiful landscaping, art features and dining options makes it a wonderful place to spend quality family time

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Always an enjoyable stop for a Saturday morning outing with family. Young kids were fascinated with how the terrapins climbed out from the pond to get a bit of sunbathing. Good opportunity for being close to and learning about nature.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
HortPark is a hidden gem that is perfectly connected to Kent Ridge Park and the Alexandra Arch, making it a great spot for a scenic walk.

The park is very well-equipped with a multi-purpose hall and several playgrounds that kids will love. For parents traveling with young children, there is a dedicated privacy room, which is a thoughtful and convenient touch.

If you are looking for a bite to eat, there are 2 or 3 restaurants on-site, including Canopy and Vineyard.

Things to note:
• The Best Part: They have water coolers throughout the park, and the water is always refreshingly cold—exactly what you need in the heat!
• Transport: There is a bus stop right outside the park entrance. nearest MRt is Labrador park MRT.
• Parking: On-site parking is available but is chargeable.

Highly recommend this park for families and hikers!

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: One of my favourite places to go for a walk when I need some quiet. Peaceful, green, and not too crowded. The gardens are beautiful and well maintained, and there are plenty of shaded paths to stroll along. It’s a nice place to slow down, clear your mind, and just enjoy being surrounded by nature. A little hidden gem in Singapore.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: This Park is both a park and a horticultural test bed centre. It has much to offer to visitors and it connected to the sort of nearby famous Henderson Waves via the Alexandra Arch Bridge and Forest Walk in that order.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
I recently attended a small group circuit training session at Trilogic Fitness in Novena and had a great experience.

The gym is spacious, fully air-conditioned, and conveniently located at the IRAS Building, just a short walk from Novena MRT station.

Eugene and his team were incredibly knowledgeable, supportive, and attentive throughout the session. They took the time to ensure everyone was performing the exercises with proper form and selected exercises that would help us build the conditioning needed for our upcoming challenge.

Their dedication, expertise, and encouragement made the session both effective and enjoyable. We are very happy with the outcome and would like to thank Eugene and the entire Trilogic Fitness team for their excellent support. Highly recommended!

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
My first time here today and was awed by the size and spaciousness of the gym. It has the best of both worlds; strength and cardio areas. Also hyrox stations if that’s your thing! Also there’s an area for physiotherapy and workout classes. It’s quite about everything you can ask for in a gym, and work out without feeling self-conscious. From the front desk to the boss himself showing us around, we felt so welcomed. There was a vending machine which intrigued me with plenty of other merchandise you can purchase on your own.

I believe it’s a place worth travelling for and they have PTs who can provide you attentive support for your fitness goals. Eugene explained the workouts and pointed out things I needed to work on to correct certain postures. He’s a great PT but if he’s not available I’m sure there are other great PTs for you to pick from.

Thank you Eugene for bringing us around and your hospitality too!

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Trilogic is such an amazing gym: well rounded for all types of training needs, impeccably clean and well-maintained equipment and facilities, and always gives 100% positive energy whenever you step inside. All the trainers are super friendly and helpful without ever being intimidating or discouraging. I’ve been going to Trilogic for more than a year and it’s been consistent in all these areas and more. I’ve also been training with their Personal Trainer, Justin, and it’s been the most rewarding training experience. Justin helped give me the knowledge, guidance, and confidence I needed to be consistent with my fitness goals. He was able to modify and adjust anything I wanted to learn so that it worked for my body and goals specifically. And he always pays special attention to any pain or injuries I have. I never realized how super rewarding having a personal trainer could be! I’ve lost a lot of weight, I’ve gained strength and flexibility, and I’ve learned how to work with both my own strengths and weaknesses to get results and efficiency out of my exercise routine. I would not have been able to do this on my own so I’m really glad I decided to try personal training and even more so that it was in such a safe and welcoming environment, one that Trilogic has created with such ease, heart, and passion. With other gyms I’ve tried I could feel it was just a ‘business’, but at Trilogic I can really see their passion and that they truly care to make each and every member’s fitness health and wellness matter

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Thank you eugene and ling for making my visit to the gym so pleasant. Eugene was knowledgeable and patient in correcting my form and giving me tips. Ling’s music is the best!!! Thank u trilogic fitness. Truely a 5/5 place. I recommend everyone to come here

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Eugene & Ling are the bessssst!
Conned my friends (& sister) to join me for a friendly group challenge (we have all never trained together before)
And Eugene was so patient with us and our lack of knowledge about the challenge even 😂
He guided us through everything and showed us the best way to execute every station!

I’ve lost a lot of weight, I’ve gained strength and flexibility, and I’ve learned how to work with both my own strengths and weaknesses to get results and efficiency out of my exercise routine. I would not have been able to do this on my own so I’m really glad I decided to try personal training and even more so that it was in such a safe and welcoming environment, one that Trilogic has created with such ease, heart, and passion. With other gyms I’ve tried I could feel it was just a ‘business’, but at Trilogic I can really see their passion and that they truly care to make each and every member’s fitness health and wellness matter

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Thank you eugene and ling for making my visit to the gym so pleasant. Eugene was knowledgeable and patient in correcting my form and giving me tips. Ling’s music is the best!!! Thank u trilogic fitness. Truely a 5/5 place. I recommend everyone to come here

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs

Eugene & Ling are the bessssst!
Conned my friends (& sister) to join me for a friendly group challenge (we have all never trained together before)
And Eugene was so patient with us and our lack of knowledge about the challenge even 😂

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: The American Club Singapore consistently delivers a great experience across both the restaurant and the gym. The restaurant offers excellent food, attentive service, and a welcoming atmosphere that makes every visit enjoyable. The gym is equally impressive—clean, well-maintained, and equipped with everything needed for a strong workout in a comfortable and professional environment. Both areas reflect a very high standard of quality and service. Highly recommend.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Good food, good service, comfortable space, and one of our best investment so far.

I was a junior member growing up in the late 2000s and am glad to have joined again as an adult member when my family moved back to Singapore. It was a safe space for my husband and I to hang out with our friends back in high school & I’m looking forward to our kids doing the same as they grow up.

We’ve utilized the facilities as young adults, as working adults with no kids, and are using more of them now that our family has grown. Staff has always been friendly & some know us personally. Prices are fair and atmosphere very comfortable especially after the renovations.

Overall thankful to have this as our second home in town.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Visited as a guest last night and was genuinely impressed. The club is lavish yet welcoming, with an atmosphere that feels refined but never intimidating.
From the moment we arrived, the Front Office staff greeted us warmly and were friendly, attentive, and professional. It was clear they know their members, which speaks volumes about the level of service and relationship they maintain.
Had a meal at the Level 1 restaurant and the food was good and well-presented. What really stood out, though, was the consistency of the service.  Even after we were done and on our way out, the Front Office staff still made it a point to greet us again and thank us for coming.
Honestly, the service felt better than many hotels. Kudos to the team for setting such a high standard. 👏

review_text: Ordered the Chicken Caesar Burrito which was no doubt of a good size but it is lacklustre in terms of taste. The Wrap was thick and chewy and cold. The chocolate layer has a tinge of sea-salt infused into the mousse, hence the umami. It is still too sweet to my taste, not for the diabetic unless you live to eat.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
The Singapore Recreation Club (SRC)
Review on 20/1/2026

The Singapore Recreation Club (SRC), founded in 1883 and set along the iconic Padang, is one of Singapore’s most historic social‑sports institutions. Its clubhouse offers a wide range of sports, recreation, dining, and event facilities that continue to serve a vibrant and diverse community.

The club’s most striking feature is its grand entrance, framed by two sweeping curved staircases. This graceful, ceremonial design creates a sense of ascending into a place of heritage and dignity. Against the backdrop of the Padang and the Civic District, the entrance stands out as a timeless and memorable architectural landmark.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Private club in central district with roofed warm and cold swimming pool, restaurant, bar with live band and cafe.
Cost environment for private gathering and easily accessible by MRT via City Hall station

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Dinner with an old friend at the SRC'S Chinese Restaurant. Reminiscent of the old days in Singapore, with traditions alive and well. Very pleasant evening 😊😋

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Our family had a wonderful visit to the Singapore Recreation Club (SRC) during their open house! The location right by the Padang is fantastic, and we were impressed that the facilities are truly top-notch. The pool looked inviting, and the entire club was clean and prestigious. It's clear why it's a desirable spot, but be aware that it is indeed for members only. We really enjoyed our temporary visit and appreciated seeing the high quality of the club.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Not a member but, their facility is quite complete. From dance, restaurant, chill drinking club, outside chill area, car parking, swimming pool, gym, to pickleball court

place_name: Agora Colearning (Preschool, Play School, Enrichment, Student Care)
review_text:
Our 14-month-old son absolutely loves his weekly Play and Bloom class and looks forward to it every week. He has thrived in their care, growing more confident and engaged through their perfectly tailored activities.

The teachers are truly wonderful—they are so patient, loving, and skilled at connecting with each child. A special highlight was the Christmas party. We were so impressed by the atmosphere; the entire place was genuinely filled with the joy and happiness of the children and their parents. It was a beautiful, festive event that everyone enjoyed.

Agora has created a warm, stimulating, and community-focused environment. We couldn't be happier!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: Agora Colearning (Preschool, Play School, Enrichment, Student Care)
review_text:
I’ve had a really positive experience with the EP coordinators. They take the time to recommend classes that truly suit each child’s level, and I really appreciate how responsive they are—especially when it comes to arranging makeup classes quickly when needed.

I also feel reassured knowing that the vendors in Agora are carefully screened, so only reputable and trustworthy providers are included.

What stands out to me most is the regular check-ins with both parents and children. It really helps me understand my child’s progress and any concerns along the way. The coordinators often go beyond their roles, and I feel they genuinely care about providing holistic support and guidance for the kids.

One small suggestion from my side would be to consider incentives for longer-term or multiple EP sign-ups. It would also be really helpful if there were better lunch arrangements in Agora, especially for students with classes starting at 2:45pm, so they have enough time to eat and rest properly before lessons. A more substantial lunch option (beyond the usual student care catering) would make the experience even better.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: Agora Colearning (Preschool, Play School, Enrichment, Student Care)
review_text:
My son has had an amazing journey with Agora Preschool right from his play school days to preschool. He made many wonderful friends, learned so many new things, and developed good manners and positive behavior along the way. He truly enjoyed coming to school every day and has grown beautifully under the care and guidance of his teachers.

We will always miss the school, his friends, and all the wonderful teachers who made his learning journey so special and memorable. Thank you for everything and for being such an important part of his early years.

Special thanks to Ms Claire for being super support and flexible throughout 🙏

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: Agora Colearning (Preschool, Play School, Enrichment, Student Care)
review_text: My child had a hard P1 transition and had a rough Q1 with school + post school care before moving to Agora. Now she is thriving in P2 and attends 4 enrichment classes with Agora. During her stay, Ms Linda fr sch care was wonderful with helping an anxious and change resistant stranger fearing child adapt and integrate to make new friends at a managed pace. Yenn fr enrichment has been an awesome parent support. She arranges enrichment, make ups, checks up on kid, intervenes when my child/ kids arrives disregulated and in tears. She's always sitting there, always comforts her and is able partner enrichment teachers to ease my kid into classes on bad days (which was half of P1). Missed class refunds are arranged automatically, new interesting classes are sent for consideration. As a working parent, I can focus and I know my kid is well cared for at the center and spelling/ ting Xie is revised in Agora. I can reach out to Yenn anytime and the team is responsive and attentive to parents and kids needs. Thank you so much :)

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text:
Visited the National Library Building mainly for The Albatross File: Singapore’s Independence Declassified exhibition, and it turned out to be a meaningful experience. The exhibition itself is relatively small, but the historical significance is immense. It provides a fascinating and candid look into the events leading up to Singapore’s separation from Malaysia in 1965, including documents and notes showing that both sides had, by then, come to see separation as the likely path forward. Seeing the actual letters, transcripts, and handwritten notes made this pivotal chapter of Singapore’s history feel very real and personal

I also took some time to roam around the Lee Kong Chian Reference Library and was impressed by how incredibly well managed the entire place is. The building itself is architecturally striking, modern, spacious, and filled with natural light. The library environment feels calm, organized, and conducive for learning and research, with clear signage and well-maintained facilities throughout

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text: The National Library is conveniently located with good traffic access from around the island. It’s a comfy cool place to be if your want to study, research and just reading. You may spent hours but no worries about hunger as the are many choices of food in the area.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text: The Albatross File exhibition at NLB is a fascinating showcase of Singapore’s separation history, featuring declassified documents, oral histories, and immersive multimedia displays. It brings to life the tense years leading up to independence, blending archival records with dramatized storytelling.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text:
2026 January - Did quick look around, enjoyed reading the stories from the mini exhibition Knots of Time. Looks like there are other exhibitions and all sorts of collection, will be back to explore and read.

Great ambience, nice for researchers and the public alike. Place has plenty of space if you'd like to get some work done, offers brilliant views, and even has a skygarden. Power and water is readily available, and theres even sufficient space for a stroll if you're feeling a mental block coming on.

Will be back to check out the albatross files. The atrium at the bottom is very pleasant, and the vaguely 'heritage' old chang kee seems like a good place to check out as well.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text:
Albatross exhibition of Singapore

Thankful for this curated history that bares all that Singaporeans may not know. We had the privilege of having seniors in their 70s who came along, cementing our understanding of what they had gone thru.

The whole setup allowed us to go at our own pace and the voice of our late and retired ministers add a sense of urgency and realism.

A suggestion for those who are not English educated, as the ministers are speaking in English, please allow translator set made avail in various language to our non English speaking seniors.

Lastly the wooden board is super heavy. Made it very difficult for us to lift up the board to read the files.  Lastly is there a need to have the board hidden? Most don’t know it exist until someone lifted it up.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Tucked within the vibrant heart of Tekka Food Centre, 蒸好味 Steamed Fish Cuisine is a quiet ode to the beauty of honest, well-executed cooking.

What sets this humble gem apart is its unwavering commitment to freshness — each fish is live-caught for the day, delivering a delicate sweetness that simply cannot be replicated. Gently steamed to perfection, the flesh remains tender and succulent, elevated only by a refined touch of ginger and scallion that enhances rather than overpowers. Every bite speaks of clarity, balance, and respect for the ingredient.

Equally noteworthy is their steamed egg — a silky, comforting masterpiece crafted from real eggs cracked fresh on the spot, not pre-mixed liquid substitutes. The result is a custard-like texture that is impossibly smooth, rich, and deeply satisfying.

In a setting as bustling as Tekka, this stall stands out not through extravagance, but through precision, sincerity, and an unwavering dedication to quality. A true hidden indulgence for those who appreciate the quiet luxury of simplicity done exceptionally well.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
The food never disappoints—every bite hits the spot. While the service is decent, there’s still room to elevate the overall experience. If you want to enjoy it at its best, come before lunchtime… unless you don’t mind a long, hungry wait in line.
And once you’re seated, don’t hold back—order the duck webs, pig ears, and duck wings. Paired with an ice-cold beer, it’s the kind of combo that keeps you reaching for more.
One visit is all it takes—you’ll already be planning your next.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: The first time I tried the food stalls here was on a Sunday afternoon. It was crowded and I got overwhelmed. I came back on a Wednesday and had tremendous service even though we barely spoke the same language.  Food was delicious. I found a clean spot to sit and eat and soak in the atmosphere.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Actually went there to try again the famous 545 whampoa prawn noodle. Saw this stall (several stall from 545). An old amah alone selling. Surprisingnya the food taste good. And worth the price for a bowl like this.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Did a market walk at Tekka Centre — a true slice of Singapore life! 🇸🇬

First built in 1915 as Kandang Kerbau Market, this Little India landmark has evolved into a vibrant multi-use hub:

Ground/Level 1 – bustling dry/wet market & food centre with fresh produce & multi-ethnic eats - spoilt for choice!

Level 2 – heritage shops with colourful textiles & hidden gems

B1 – convenient parking below

My Favorite stop: Alhajar Latifa (#01-116) — where curry spices are freshly blended just for you. Always greeted by cheerful & pleasant staff.

Will be back to explore more of Level 2 soon!😻

The food never disappoints—every bite hits the spot. While the service is decent, there’s still room to elevate the overall experience. If you want to enjoy it at its best, come before lunchtime… unless you don’t mind a long, hungry wait in line.
And once you’re seated, don’t hold back—order the duck webs, pig ears, and duck wings. Paired with an ice-cold beer, it’s the kind of combo that keeps you reaching for more.
One visit is all it takes—you’ll already be planning your next.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: The first time I tried the food stalls here was on a Sunday afternoon. It was crowded and I got overwhelmed. I came back on a Wednesday and had tremendous service even though we barely spoke the same language.  Food was delicious. I found a clean spot to sit and eat and soak in the atmosphere.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Actually went there to try again the famous 545 whampoa prawn noodle. Saw this stall (several stall from 545). An old amah alone selling. Surprisingnya the food taste good. And worth the price for a bowl like this.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Did a market walk at Tekka Centre — a true slice of Singapore life! 🇸🇬

First built in 1915 as Kandang Kerbau Market, this Little India landmark has evolved into a vibrant multi-use hub:

Ground/Level 1 – bustling dry/wet market & food centre with fresh produce & multi-ethnic eats - spoilt for choice!

Level 2 – heritage shops with colourful textiles & hidden gems

B1 – convenient parking below

My Favorite stop: Alhajar Latifa (#01-116) — where curry spices are freshly blended just for you. Always greeted by cheerful & pleasant staff.

Will be back to explore more of Level 2 soon!😻

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Staff is just ignoring you. Asked for a menu at the bar two times - the lady said “give me a minute” and never came back. And it’s not because they’re super busy - they just don’t care. This is simply disrespectful

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Restaurant have several hit and miss cuisines but the most recent visit had me trying the fish and fries. It was really good and affordable. The fish was not overly battered and there were thick flakes of fish within that is light and fluffy!

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Singapore Swimming Club has a great selection of Indian food and have always enjoyed the food, especially someone who is very discerning. Having said that, off late the quality of paneer dishes seems to be off. The paneer tikka is no longer soft, creamy and flavourful. I think they may have changed their supplier or switched to using frozen paneer. The management should look into it and bring back the original quality paneer.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Been coming here for years since I’ve been a kid. Always love how homely and family-friendly this place is. The eateries are fantastic, especially the cafe and Chinese restaurant. The club sports facilities are excellent, I’ve used the pool, tennis courts, bowling alley, and gym before. With the Katong Park station almost ready nearby, it will become even more accessible.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Went there for the second time as my aunt is their member. The first round tried their lunch buffet. Decent taste in many varieties. This round tried their fried hokkien mee. The taste was perfect, the portion was big. But wait for my local coffee for a long time. This really needs to improve

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
The Fort Canning Tree Tunnel is definitely one of Singapore’s most iconic photo spots. The view looking up through the spiral staircase towards the lush greenery is beautiful and well worth seeing in person. It’s a unique spot that creates stunning photos, especially on a sunny day.

The only downside is the long queue, as many visitors are waiting for their turn to take photos. Be prepared to wait, especially during peak hours. That said, most people are patient and respectful, and the final result is worth it if you enjoy photography and sightseeing.

A must-visit spot in Singapore, just come early if you want to avoid the crowds!

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Location and views are nice!
Bad experience with tourists however a lot of them are very rude and speak no English. Clearly signs that say no climbing over the edge to sit however they do this and line up for it so it causes lots of congestion especially at the bottom and on the actual stairs. Would recommend going very early to avoid crowds.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Lovely park to venture near City Hall. Visited the spice garden as well. The tunnel is very crowded with queues at ard 8am. Lots of local & foreign tourists there at the tunnel. Else where, there’s peace & quiet.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: It's ok if you want to drop by.. But if purposely to come here far away to enjoy the view, it's not really worth it. Long queue during weekend. You still have to wait in queue for individual photography during weekdays. The park is not too small.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: CROWDED - Probably the most we’ve waited in Singapore. It’s a beautiful spot but extremely difficult to take photos due to the crowds. We arrived here just gone 9am on a Saturday. Groups are sadly inconsiderate in that each person spends a number of minutes taking multiple photos stopping each time someone passes by the stairs. If you really wanted an image, earlier the better. Easy to find with public transport. We did not wait to take images on the steps as I can easily imagine this queue lasting into 45 mins+ and there are other things to see also

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: I visited here for a morning walk. The park is quite large, so you'll definitely work up a sweat walking around. I highly recommend bringing a water bottle. It's full of nature, and spotting a wild chicken was a fun experience.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Relaxing spot, perfect for a nature retreat while in the city. A lot less crowded than you'd expect, considering it's central spot.

It's a collection of spots really, rather than a single destination, with lifestyle dining, museums, educational spice gardens, wedding photo destinations, or just a plain picnic spot. It's much less of a climb than you'd think, is great all around.

View from the top is a comforting one, nice to see the cityscape.

One interesting thing is how it's occupied mainly by maids, foreign workers, and tourists. As an aside, Chinese tourists and their themed outfits are kinda fun! Maybe we should look into that. Under-utilized by Singaporeans - thats your tax dollars at work you know, might as well use it.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: This is a quiet green park in Singapore. Looks like an old park with lot of greenery. It's clam, quiet and with lot of shade its ideal for a relaxing time. The famous Fort canning tree tunnel is also located here. Entrance is free. Popular place amongst the tourists. Good place to relax.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Fort Canning Park is a lush green destination right in the heart of the city, notable for its tranquil atmosphere and historical significance. It's perfect for strolling, relaxing, and taking photos, especially with its famous "tree tunnel" viewpoint.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Refurbished recently. Some nice deco outside but not much has changed.
The net playground is still there, which is a good thing and a unique attraction to this mall.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
🟠 City Square Mall
180 Kitchener Road
Singapore 208539

🟣 Location =  🟣 the mall is located at the junction of Serangoon Road and Kitchener Road.

🟣 It is directly connected to the Farrer Park MRT station via B1 level passage-way.
______________________________________________
⬛️  Historical Context: The mall actually sits on the former site of the New World Amusement Park, which was a famous landmark in the Jalan Besar area for decades.

⬛️  When I was a child in the early 1970s, my late Peranakan father used to bring me to New World to jalan jalan.
________________________________________________
🟠 The mall had undergone a SGD50 million massive revamp from September 2023 to early 2026.

🟠  The revamp added 26,000 sq ft of space, featuring a new 24,000 sq ft "Gastro Square" Level 4 dining area, new playgrounds, and classy luxurious interior upgrades.

🟨 The perimeters ( outside stretch of the mall)
*************************************************
🟨  There is a small verdant green park at the back of the mall, where a decomissioned MRT train cabin sits. ▪︎It is a hub for educational purposes.

🟨 There is also a small Merry-Go-Round & a Tea Cup Spinning Ride, reminiscent of the rides I used to enjoy in the 1970s.

🟩 For the interior of the mall itself
************************************
➡️ Below Info ➡️  is from the lift directory panel.

🟩L1 = City Green

🟩 B1 = Underpass to MRT, Taxi Stand, Pick-Up & Drop-Off, Green Square/ NTUC Fairprice/ Q&M Medical & Dental

🟩 B2=  ATM / Customer Service Counter/ Don Donki

🟩 B3=  Car Park + Food Square + Food Republic

🟩 B4= Car Park

⬛️ L9 To L6 = Schools & Offices  + Singapore Sustainability Academy (SSA)

⬛️ L5 = Discovery Square & Golden Village Cinema

⬛️ L4=  Gastro Square & CONNECT @ City Square Mall ( 🔴5 social service agencies)

⬛️ L 3 =  MST Golf Super Store

⬛️ L2 =  Decathlon & Uniqlo
________________________________________
🔴 Important info:
*******************
🔴 Level 4 features a CONNECT at City Square Mall, a dedicated community space comprising 5 ( five) social service agencies, affiliated with NCSS ( ▪︎National Council of Social Services), which itself is a statutory board under MSF.

🔴 MSF = Ministry of Social and Family Development.

🔴 The five agencies on Level 4 are
**************************************
● Counselling and Care Centre
● Kampong Kapor Community Services
● Singapore Children’s Society
● The Salvation Army

● Eagles Mediation & Counselling Centre ➡️ at B2 level

➖️ Dated 24 March 2026/ Tuesday.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
If you are around Little India area, City Square Mall is actually a pretty nice place to chill and makan.
It's just a short walk from Mustafa Centre and the mall has a good mix of food spots, from quick bites to proper restaurants and food court options. Halal food choices are quite easy to find too.

Shopping wise, it's the kind of mall that feels practical and comfortable. Got everyday stores like Uniqlo and Decathlon, so you can actually shop without everything feeling overly expensive.

Bonus point for tea lovers… theres Beutea too🧋

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
It’s a nice mall and came here because to buy a drink from the nice supermarket. There is a Rest n Go area where there are many massage chairs. You have to scan the QR code to make the payment first and select which package. It’s worth as paying for $15 and 70mins of relaxation! Even paying $10 and 45mins of relaxation is good too. There were many willing to pay and relax on the chairs here and that’s good.

Latest update! A wonderful discovery and if you’re looking for a barber nearby this mall, there’s one! Jerudili Barber and it’s operated by a passionate with professionalism guy. He takes care of your hair well and really comfortable. He cuts hair great too! Please do support since it’s quite new. It’s at 681 Race Course Road and exit F, once alighted from Farrer Park MRT station. I have attached the barber shop photo too which includes the address.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
It’s a very big mall with many renowned international brands like Levi’s, Breadtalk, and toy stores for the little ones like ToysRUs etc. Also sometimes there is a flea market inside the mall with some cool toys and books for a great price. Lots of restaurants as well in easy access.

Outside there is a musical park where you can play some soothing music or relax on the hammocks and there is also a eco bus, with environmental activities and it also spreads awareness about the environment. In all, it’s a great mall!

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
It’s a nice mall and came here because to buy a drink from the nice supermarket. There is a Rest n Go area where there are many massage chairs. You have to scan the QR code to make the payment first and select which package. It’s worth as paying for $15 and 70mins of relaxation! Even paying $10 and 45mins of relaxation is good too. There were many willing to pay and relax on the chairs here and that’s good.

Latest update! A wonderful discovery and if you’re looking for a barber nearby this mall, there’s one! Jerudili Barber and it’s operated by a passionate with professionalism guy. He takes care of your hair well and really comfortable. He cuts hair great too! Please do support since it’s quite new. It’s at 681 Race Course Road and exit F, once alighted from Farrer Park MRT station. I have attached the barber shop photo too which includes the address.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
It’s a very big mall with many renowned international brands like Levi’s, Breadtalk, and toy stores for the little ones like ToysRUs etc. Also sometimes there is a flea market inside the mall with some cool toys and books for a great price. Lots of restaurants as well in easy access.

Outside there is a musical park where you can play some soothing music or relax on the hammocks and there is also a eco bus, with environmental activities and it also spreads awareness about the environment. In all, it’s a great mall!

review_text:
Refurbished recently. Some nice deco outside but not much has changed.

There is also sbs transit and SJE bus operating from this terminal

Accessibility: 400 meter to Bugis MRT (EWL/Green Line/ DWL Blue Line) and also can access to Jalan Besar MRT (DWL/ Blue Line)

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: To get to JB from here is easy. If you want to sit in the bus, line up on the right side but if you don’t kind standing, line up on the left side.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Why is there nowhere to sit?
Now some days later after arriving at Kuala Lumpur’s beautiful bus terminal, the comparison to Singapore’s terminal is extreme. KL’s is like a modern airport terminal, Singapore’s Queen Street is more like a empty boot sale

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Overall, the transportation itself was smooth and we had no issues with the ride. However, our experience at the station was quite disappointing due to the attitude of one of the staff members.
We were traveling with a young child and accidentally walked into the station area because we did not notice the signage, which was quite small and easy to miss. Instead of guiding us in a calm and helpful manner, an elderly staff member raised his voice at us in a way that felt unnecessary and unfriendly.
We completely understand that there are rules to follow, and we had no intention of going the wrong way. A polite explanation would have been much more appropriate, especially considering we were with a child.
We hope the staff can be reminded to communicate more kindly with visitors. A little patience and understanding can make a big difference in creating a welcoming environment for everyone.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
The terminal itself is easy to find, but my experience with the security staff was very poor. While crossing a zebra crossing with no buses entering or exiting, a guard revved his engine at me. He then aggressively asked if I could speak English and if I was "blind," pointing to a sign. When I pointed out it was a zebra crossing, he shouted that the rule was only for bus drivers.

According to Singapore's Highway Code, pedestrians have the right of way on pedestrian crossings. The guard's aggressive and incorrect interpretation of the rules makes pedestrians feel unsafe. Management urgently needs to retrain its security team on basic traffic rules and respectful communication.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: The school has a warm and inviting atmosphere with an expansive outdoor area - big plus point ! The teachers and staff in Etonhouse Newton are  nurturing, kind and approachable. That definitely shows in how the children are settled, always cheerful & engaged! Thank you for the wonderful experience

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Our kid has been at this kindergarten for almost a year, and we’re so glad we chose it. At first there was a bit of separation anxiety, but now they’re always excited to go every morning. The teachers are super caring and patient, and the environment feels really warm. We can see our child becoming more confident, independent, and caring — it really feels like a second home for them.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
We have had a truly wonderful experience at EtonHouse Newton. The school provides a nurturing and encouraging environment where children feel seen, heard, and loved.

We are deeply grateful to the dedicated teachers and community for the care, guidance, and warmth they show every day. Thank you for shaping happy little humans 💛

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
My experience with EtonHouse Newton has been nothing short of positive. The space felt calm, welcoming, and thoughtfully put together.

Nothing seemed without intention—which reflects the school’s commitment to the IB curriculum, not just in theory but in how it shapes daily interactions and learning experiences.

The classrooms were among my favourite parts of the school. Each had its own style, highlighting the individuality of both teachers and children—something I wish I had experienced more of growing up.

Altogether, it left me with the impression of a place that truly values not just how children learn, but how they feel while learning. I’d highly recommend dropping by, you’ll meet some of the kindest people I’ve ever come across.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
As a family PA, I frequently communicated with EtonHouse Newton because my boss’s child attends the school.  I’ve witnessed the remarkable growth of my boss’s daughter into a sweet, loving, and respectful young woman under EtonHouse’s care.

The teachers at EtonHouse Newton are truly exceptional. They are caring, attentive, and nurturing, and their operations, parent liaison, and education are exceptionally impressive.  Every staff member at EtonHouse Newton, including the helpful admin personnel like Ms. Keisha, is prompt and professional in responding to my inquiries about school matters.

Thank you, EtonHouse, for shaping our little princess and her elder brother into the wonderful individuals they are today. Your hard work in preparing their foundation has undoubtedly played a crucial role in their future success.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: The mutation orchid garden is truly stunning and unique, filled with rare and vibrant blooms that capture attention at every corner. Each orchid displays unusual colors, intricate patterns, and elegant shapes, creating a magical atmosphere for visitors. The garden is carefully arranged, allowing guests to walk through and admire the beauty up close. However, the strong sunlight makes the visit slightly tiring, especially during midday. Despite the heat, the breathtaking scenery and peaceful environment make the experience worthwhile. With a bit more shade or a cooler time of day, the visit would be even more enjoyable and relaxing for everyone.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Been here many times.  Still think it is a good place to relax.
The garden is very well planned and full of greenery.
There is also a new indoor garden air-conditioning space where you can enjoy completely different species of orchids.
If it rains, there are many places to hide.  There are also drinks vending machines.
Even if you need to pay in the park, I suggest you come.  It's worth it!👍

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Absolutely spectacular! These garden were Beautiful and very well put together. Clearly a lot of thought has been put into the displays. They also had spots specifically set up for photo opportunities which helps to reduce clogging on the paths. There are bathrooms and places to get refreshments near by. There are also shaded areas throughout the gardens where you can shelter from the rain or sun as needed.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Well manicured and maintained, this place is. The design of this part of Botanic gardens takes you on a journey through meandering pathways and cooling glasshouse. Certainly worth the entrance fee. Orchids are one of the most intriguing flowers. As the old adage goes necessity is the mother of evolution, these orchids are perfectly evolved to benefit from their symbiotic pollinators. Some uses trickery to entice the male species of insects to land on the stigma, some make the landing pad comfortable for a nice sip of delicious nectar. Amazing!

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Absolutely stunning the highlight of our trip for me. An incredible number of beautiful orchids in a lovely landscaped and meandering garden. Entry is just 5 dollars making this easily one of the most affordable things to do in Singapore, and absolutely worthwhile. You could easily spend a day wandering the orchid garden and the surrounding botanic gardens

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Never seen a more beautiful MRT station that incorporates nature even above it. Newton MRT is very special and gives a luxurious vibe even from walking along the footpaths. Pretty expensive area in Singapore and truly different.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Passed by this station. It was, as usual, clean and immaculate throughout the MRT, which includes the toiletries. Many places you can go to from this MRT stop. Overall, a nice and comfortable atmosphere at this MRT station.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Another clean, efficient Singapore metro station.  This one is particularly convenient as it serves the blue and red lines, making it a popular interchange when travelling downtown.

These stations are so efficient and most people using them are very considerate.  They all make Singapore a great and easy place to travel around.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: 26 June 2025 I'm here at 00:12 for taking North-South line and the station have no air with heat, I guess air-con off early. Please understand for peoples waiting for next train.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: I went here almost every day during my vacation in Singapore. Clean, structured and also pretty convenient (walking distance from the hotels and plaza)

review_text:
This hawker center was the first place I visited right after I landed in Singapore. Personally, I cannot say that this was the best in Singapore since I have not yet tried other places at the time.

As a first time traveler, Newton Hawkers were so welcoming! I had a great time with the vibes by the uncles and aunties offering their products to me. Majority of the crowd are also local, which is a plus for me. This specific stall (in the picture) was the best (service-wise).

In the photo, I have made friends with Auntie Nico and Uncle John. I don’t know their relationship or if they own the store but they treated me so well and even offered to show me how they cook their food (I told them that I own a restaurant and was curious about how they cook their food).

In the end of the night, I asked them to take a picture with me and they did. Auntie Nico also used her phone to get a selfie with me lol. Fun times!

There’s always something beautiful about hard working people and everytime you get a chance to talk to them, do it!

WILL ALWAYS VISIT HERE EVERYTIME I GO TO SINGAPORE!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Newton Food Centre is a must-visit in Singapore. Lively, authentic, and packed with incredible food options, it’s everything you want from a classic hawker centre.

The variety is outstanding — from local favourites to late-night comfort dishes, there’s something for everyone. Prices are very reasonable, portions are generous, and the flavours are bold and authentic. It’s the kind of place where you can try multiple dishes in one visit and still want to come back again.

The atmosphere is busy and energetic, especially in the evenings, but that’s part of the charm. Despite the crowds, there’s plenty of seating and the space is well laid out. Being open 24 hours is a huge bonus, whether you’re after a late supper, early breakfast, or anything in between.

Great spot for travellers wanting a genuine local experience, groups with different tastes, or anyone keen to eat well without breaking the bank. Casual, vibrant, and full of character — exactly what a Singapore hawker centre should be.

🍜 Huge variety of food
🌶️ Authentic local flavours
💰 Affordable prices
⏰ Open 24 hours
🎉 Lively atmosphere

Highly recommended.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
• Food
The food selection at this center is a reliable choice for local flavors. The chicken wings and satay are particular highlights, especially when paired with a cold beverage. Another notable mention is the Gong Gong, which is a dish that often prompts a visit to this location. The flavors are authentic and provide a satisfying taste of local seafood and grilled items.

• Service & Atmosphere
The atmosphere is lively and typical of a popular open-air food center in Singapore. It offers a vibrant setting that is suitable for casual gatherings. While the environment can get busy and crowded, it remains a practical and energetic spot for a late-night meal or a social drink. The service varies by stall but is generally efficient and functional.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Well known hawker centre that once upon a time catered mostly to tourists.

But locals are now crowding the place because of the good food and the variety and because the Singapore Tourism Board has successfully clamped down some seafood stalls touting and scamming patrons, especially tourists, with exorbitant prices.

This is one hawker centre where you will find people from all over the world savouring local street food of all races in Singapore which originated from China, India,  Malaysia, Indonesia, Thailand,  etc and even the US.

Beer by the jugs are served here.

Most stalls are closed for lunch. But it is usually quite difficult to get a table during dinner time, especially on weekends. Sharing table is the norm.  So be willing to do so.

Queuing at some stalls to collect your own order is also the norm.

This hawker centre, including the free toilets, are very clean and airy. There are basins all over for patrons to wash their hands before and after meals
meals.

Pay when you order. In cash or via your phone transfer.

There is an adjoining carpark. A huge one. But it can still be hard to find a parking space during dinner time.

The Newton MRT is very near by.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: A true foodie paradise, with flavors from across Singapore, Malaysia, Thailand, and China all coming together in one place. You’ll find yourself eating alongside both locals and tourists, which adds to the energy and authenticity. Not too crowded on a Monday night, making it easy to explore. Highlights included the fresh sugar cane juice, black pepper crab, Singapore chili stingray, fried squid, and the classic Singapore “carrot cake.” A great way to experience the diversity of the region’s street food culture.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Woodlands Waterfront Park is a relaxing and scenic park located in the northern part of Singapore. It faces the Straits of Johor, so visitors can actually see Malaysia across the water. The park covers around 11 hectares and has a 1.5-kilometre waterfront promenade, which makes it a great place for walking, jogging, or cycling while enjoying the sea breeze.
One of the most interesting features of the park is the 400-metre Woodlands Waterfront Jetty, which is the northernmost point of mainland Singapore. Walking along the jetty is a really nice experience because you get a wide view of the sea and the coastline. Many people go there to fish, take photos, or just relax while looking at the view. In the evening, the sunset from the jetty is especially beautiful and creates a calm atmosphere.
In my opinion, the park is a great place to visit if you want to take a break from the busy city. The long paths are perfect for a peaceful walk, and the cool sea breeze makes the environment very comfortable. There are also open spaces where families can have picnics, as well as a playground where children can play and climb.
Another thing I like about the park is that it is usually less crowded compared to many other parks in Singapore. Because of this, it feels quieter and more relaxing. Overall, Woodlands Waterfront Park is a wonderful place to enjoy nature, spend time with family and friends, and appreciate the beautiful waterfront scenery.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
This seaside park has an interesting history. The jetty, which is the centerpiece of the park, was a mooring location for the Malayan/Malaysian Navy when Singapore was still within the federation. It's surprising that the plot of land was still in the hand of Malaysian government till it's handed over in 1980s (almost 20 years upon the secession). The bollards at the jetty point out to the place's not-so-distant past.

Since the construction of the RTS train, it has been pretty popular place for the locals to take selfie, with the backdrop of the viaduct. Funnily enough, in spite of advisory against fishing at the jetty (posted all over), seems this is just a live and let live policy lol.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: It's a beautiful place we can see Singapore and Malaysia at that same time. When I went it's full rainy and I took photoshoot there. We want to spend peace area I will suggest The Woodlands Waterfront. Beautiful silent area. We will hear only Air sound, Water sound.

review_text:

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

Woodlands Waterfront Park is one of my favorite spots to unwind. The park offers a beautiful, relaxing view of the Johor Strait, especially during sunset. The waterfront promenade is perfect for a peaceful stroll or a bike ride, and there’s plenty of open space for picnics with family and friends.

I also love the iconic jetty—it’s a great place to take in the sea breeze and watch the boats go by. The park is well-maintained, with clean paths and plenty of benches to sit and enjoy the scenery. Highly recommended for anyone looking to escape the city hustle without going too far!

It is quite hot in the afternoon so pls plan your trip here in the morning where it is not so hot. There is some bus stop near here but you can also walk here from woodlands mrt throguh Admiralty park. Not many facilities here except for the seafood restaurant and just greenery around.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: One-North is a very well-structured MRT station, perfectly servicing one of Singapore's major business and industrial districts. The layout is refreshingly straightforward, making it a station where you're unlikely to ever be late due to navigation issues. With multiple exits that integrate directly into the surrounding buildings, it’s a 'get the job done' kind of stop that feels custom-built for the busy professionals working in the area.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: You would love the nice aroma when you come out of the train in the morning. Still can't figure out from which store. A lot of good food options. Cold storage at the MRT level.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: New, clean, convenient access MRT station which connects to the circle line (yellow line). There are more and more restaurants, bars  opened nearby, you could also find stationary shop and clinic.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: I think it is quite a remote area in the west. However, the food and other amenities are fairly good, and it is a relatively concentrated office district.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Christ oriented hotel which needs some TLC.

I did appreciate that this Hotel belongs to a Christian organization and that they value community and are God fearing. However, room upkeep needs more work. The rooms are not as clean as they could be, especially seats and carpet stained.

I watched the young boy change my sheets on the third day and he was putting our pillow cases on dirty surfaces.

The gym and pool have strange opening and closing times. These facilities should be freely available 24/7 to suit patrons. You also need to go to the counter to swap your room card each time you want to go.

The buffet breakfast is a real let down with really basic food choices that are the same every single day. I just think they could put a bit more love into it.

Overall, a decent location which is close to Orchard and 5 minute walk to Stevens MRT.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Metropolitan YMCA Singapore offers a pleasant and practical stay just a short distance from Orchard Road. The property is spacious, clean, and reasonably priced for an NGO-run hotel. It’s conveniently connected, with easy transport to Orchard and an MRT station within walking distance, and provides all the necessary amenities for a comfortable stay.

A highlight is its cozy café, which serves dishes made with fresh Hydroponics-grown vegetables. The hotel also reflects a strong social mission, showing inclusivity by employing people with special needs, and offers added conveniences like an automated e-mart.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Pleasantly surprised at how awesome this place was, enjoyed our room, location close to Orchard. We will definitely come back!! Thank you!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Just okay.  We were surprised how dirty our room was.  The "free" WiFi is so bad, we had to buy a separate date plan with the data app Saily.  The pool is nice.  The staff is a mix, some are really nice and helpful, but others are uninterested in helping with anything.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_author_name: ひきこもり。高橋正臣。Masaomi Eric Takahashi.
review_text:
My son and his mum has been staying here and I joined my son occasionally. Except for the helpful and friendly Front staffs, Andrew and Karen, the rest of the other management team hiding inside their offices are the vilest and most hostile employees in this establishment. Unprofessional. No greetings from the woman who confronted me about bringing outside food into the hotel premises!? No greetings, No Hello. She just march right up to me then started chastising me and vehemently rebutted me. She complained to Karen about my presence and spared no expense screeching outside the lobby to let me hear her displeasure.
Subsequently, all the other management staffs were notified of my presence and one by one they came out of their office just to antagonise me! They sure made it very personal and it sure felt personal, disingenuous and contumelious!
I left and my son would never grace YMCA Stevens again, such shamelessly unprofessional and hostile management staffs!
Father and son definitely DO NOT RECOMMEND this place.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: I was here today (17 DECEMBER 2025) arriving via the Downtown Line, to switch over to the North South Line. While this MRT Station is quite an old one, it is well maintained and the ambience therein is comfortable in that it is well ventilated. To me, this MRT Station or interchange allows me to go to some of my favourite places in Singapore on the North South Line - in particular, Novena, Toa Payoh, Orchard and Somerset.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Short transit betw Newton Downtown Line (Blue) and North South Line (Red). Hair cut place at Blue line! Very convenient. Visit them soon :))

The red line station can be warm sometimes due to crowding & aircon under service. Friendly SMRT staff.

Landmarks: Newton Food Centre (famous for BBQ seafood, prawn mee, duck noodles, Thai food, Nasi Padang etc.)

Prudential@Scotts, NEA, Sheraton Towers, Alliance France.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Thoroughly enjoy the Newton MRT station. It's probably one of the most important station connecting people from the West side of the country to the Central zones. The downtown line is probably one of the best addition to the rail system. Efficient, clean, and not a very long walk from the Blue to the Red lines, compared to some other interchanges.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Clean, well maintained and modern MRT station in the heart of Singapore.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
After opening downtown line, the Newton circus area becomes very convenient. It’s easy to across the road toward each direction now.
(If you push the stroller or use wheelchair, you still need to check how to across safely)
But around MRT, there’s nothing special except the food court.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Orchard MRT station is one of the ‘classic’ older station in one of the earlier MRT lines in Singapore.

It's a shopping hub, a ‘must-see’ destination for tourists where all things happen. I think it still retains its status.

There are big chains of departments stores like Takashimaya, Tangs, Wisma Atria, and many hotels around here. If you want a leg cramping shopping experience, walk from here to City Hall.

It's tidier now compared to then, imho, although it caters much more to commercial. Back then, there were more bookstores and others, now it's mostly fashion and lifestyle.

It's always crowded and noisy, sometimes going to food court feels like battle royale. If you like people and shopping, you'd love Orchard.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Orchard MRT Station has been around for decades, since I was literally a small kid. Wonderful this station remains clean, and even more accessible than before with underground and ground level connections to so many places around downtown Orchard Road. This stations serves both the red North South Line and the brown Thomson East Coast Line, which makes it extra convenient. It may be a bit busy during peak hours so just watch your step. There are plenty of friendly staff to assist if needed too.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
One of the more crowded train stations around the island. It's very conveniently located in the heart of the city, linking up neighboring shopping malls, and connected train lines, all in one single space. They're also very handicap friendly.

Just be mindful that the crowd can be quite a handful during peak hours.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Orchard MRT Station is an interchange station of North South Line and Thomson East Coast Line of Singapore MRT system. It offers seamless transfer between both lines as commuters just need to move among  platforms.

The station was clean and comfortable with good conditioning system. It has public toilet for commuters. Clear signages were stated at the station to help commuters to change between both lines or to look for the nearest exits or buildings or places. As this Orchard MRT station is located at the Singapore shopping heaven, Orchard Road, it has direct connections to shopping mall nearby.
